name: PT-141
subtitle: Melanocortin Receptor Agonist | Sexual Dysfunction Treatment
overview: FDA-approved melanocortin receptor agonist for treatment of hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women. Works centrally in the nervous system to trigger sexual arousal pathways independent of vascular mechanisms, unlike traditional ED medications.
mechanism: Selectively activates melanocortin receptors (MC3R/MC4R) in the central nervous system, triggering sexual arousal pathways independent of peripheral vascular mechanisms. Works within 45 minutes with effects lasting 6-12 hours.
keyBenefits: Effective for both male and female sexual dysfunction
Central mechanism (not dependent on blood flow)

items.name: Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (HSDD)
items.description: FDA-approved for treatment of HSDD in premenopausal women.

items.name: Erectile Dysfunction
items.description: Effective in men including PDE5 inhibitor-resistant cases via CNS pathways.

items.name: Female Sexual Arousal Disorder
items.description: Enhances sexual arousal and desire in women.

items.name: Sexual Distress Reduction
items.description: Reduces distress related to low sexual desire.

items.name: Sexual Satisfaction
items.description: Enhanced satisfaction reported in clinical trials.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the FDA-approved route. Administered 45-60 minutes before anticipated sexual activity.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial tops with alcohol swabs
Inject BAC water slowly down vial side
Gently swirl until fully dissolved (do not shake)
Solution should appear clear and colorless

keyBenefits: Abdomen 2 inches from navel or upper thigh

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal spray with faster onset (30-45 minutes). Limited clinical data compared to injectable.

interactions.notes: PT-141 works centrally; PDE5 inhibitors work peripherally - monitor for additive BP effects.
Both are melanocortin agonists; combining risks excessive activation and side effects.
PT-141 can transiently lower blood pressure - monitor closely.
Both lower BP and cause flushing - limit intake to avoid excessive hypotension.
Risk of severe hypotension - contraindicated with heart condition medications.
May work synergistically for sexual dysfunction through different pathways.
Different pathways (melanocortin vs GnRH) - no receptor overlap but monitor BP effects.

timeline.effects: Mild nausea or facial flushing possible
Onset of effects - increased arousal and desire

stopSigns: Significant blood pressure drop or dizziness
monitoring: Take anti-nausea medication 30 minutes prior if prone to nausea
Avoid alcohol to minimize BP effects

good: FDA-approved pharmaceutical grade (Vyleesi) from licensed pharmacy
Proper labeling with concentration and purity
warning: Compounded versions - ensure pharmacy is licensed and follows USP standards
bad: Colored or oily appearance indicating impurities or degradation

references.title: Bremelanotide for the Treatment of Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder: Two Randomized Phase 3 Trials (RECONNECT)
references.authors: Kingsberg SA, Clayton AH, Portman D, et al.
references.keyFindings: Two identical phase 3, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials (NCT02333071 and NCT02338960). Bremelanotide 1.75mg SubQ significantly improved sexual desire and reduced related distress in premenopausal women with HSDD.

references.title: Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Evaluation of Intranasal PT-141 in Healthy Males and Patients with Erectile Dysfunction
references.authors: Diamond LE, Earle DC, Rosen RC, et al.
references.keyFindings: Intranasal PT-141 produced dose-dependent increase in erectile activity at doses >7mg, with onset in ~30 minutes. Safe and well-tolerated in both healthy men and ED patients.

references.title: The Neurobiology of Bremelanotide for the Treatment of HSDD in Premenopausal Women
references.authors: Clayton AH, Kingsberg SA, Goldstein I, et al.
references.keyFindings: Bremelanotide activates MC4R in the medial preoptic area of the hypothalamus, increasing dopamine release. CNS mechanism of action independent of peripheral vascular effects.

references.title: Salvage of Sildenafil Failures with Bremelanotide: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study
references.keyFindings: 342 men with ED unresponsive to sildenafil randomized to 10mg bremelanotide intranasal spray vs placebo. Demonstrated effectiveness in PDE5 inhibitor-resistant cases.

latestResearch.title: Safety Profile of Bremelanotide Across the Clinical Development Program
latestResearch.summary: Comprehensive safety analysis across all bremelanotide clinical trials. Most treatment-emergent adverse events were mild/moderate, with nausea (40%), flushing (20%), and headache (11%) being most common.

latestResearch.title: Bremelanotide for Treatment of Female Hypoactive Sexual Desire
latestResearch.summary: Review of bremelanotide's role as the only as-needed FDA-approved treatment for HSDD, with evidence supporting significant improvement in desire and reduced distress.

quickStats.typicalDose: Women: 1.75mg (FDA-approved); Men: 1-2mg; Start 0.5mg test dose for tolerance
quickStats.frequency: As needed before sexual activity; max 1 dose per 24 hours
quickStats.cycleDuration: Use as needed for sexual enhancement
quickStats.storage: Refrigerate at 2-8°C, use reconstituted solution within 30 days
